meta.title: Freelance Full-Stack Developer - José Miguel Fernández
meta.description: Freelance full-stack developer in Spain. I build business websites, internal tools, automation workflows, API integrations and maintainable backend systems.

page.title: Simple, reliable software
page.intro: I help founders, solo professionals and small teams turn a concrete need into a website, internal tool, automation workflow, API integration or maintainable backend system.

about.title: Direct collaboration, technical judgment and backend depth.
about.p1: I’m a full-stack developer based in Granada, Spain. My strongest foundation is backend development with Java, Spring Boot and production systems.
about.p2: I also work across frontend, automation, CI/CD, Docker and Kubernetes when that helps ship something useful. I do not sell fixed packages: first I understand the workflow, then I build the piece that actually needs to exist.

build.title: Services to improve your website, automate work and build useful tools.
build.text: From WordPress and WooCommerce to integrations, automation and applied AI. I choose the solution around the problem, not around a fashionable technology.

skills.text: Backend is my base, but I can move across the stack when the project needs it.

cta.title: Want to know if it is worth building?

cookies.bannerText: I use cookies to offer you the best experience on this website. You can learn more about which cookies I use or disable them in the
cookies.settingsDescription: You can accept or reject analytics. Necessary cookies are always active so the site can remember your preferences and keep working.
cookies.necessaryDescription: They save this decision in your browser and keep basic website features working.
cookies.analyticsDescription: They help me understand which pages are visited and improve the content.

meta.title: Privacy and cookies
meta.description: Information about necessary cookies, Google Analytics with consent, and how to change your preferences on jomiferse.com.

privacy.title: Privacy and cookies
privacy.intro: This website uses a simple setup: what is needed for it to work, and analytics only if you accept it.

sections.title: Necessary cookies
sections.body: Necessary cookies or storage are used to remember local preferences, such as the visual theme and your cookie decision. They are not used for advertising or profiling.

sections.title: Google Analytics
sections.body: Google Analytics loads only if you accept analytics. Before your decision, and if you reject it, analytics consent stays denied.

sections.title: Changing preferences
sections.body: You can reopen the preferences panel from the cookies link in the footer and change your decision whenever you want.

sections.title: Where the decision is stored
sections.body: The preference is stored in localStorage in your browser. If you clear site data, the banner will appear again.

meta.title: About - José Miguel Fernández
meta.description: About José Miguel Fernández, a full-stack developer with strong backend foundations, focused on practical software, automation and web products.

page.title: Full-stack developer with a
page.intro: I build websites, tools, automations and backend systems that are simple to use and easy to maintain.
page.backgroundTitle: Backend depth, product sense and a practical way of building.
page.backgroundP1: I currently work on production backend systems with Java, Spring Boot, distributed architecture, CI/CD and observability.
page.backgroundP2: Before that, I worked across full-stack delivery in edtech. That mix shaped how I build: understand the need, choose the right level of system and keep it easy to evolve.

blocks.title: Backend Development
blocks.body: APIs, services, data models and integrations with Java, Spring Boot and production reliability in mind.

blocks.title: Frontend Development
blocks.body: Clean interfaces with Astro, React and TypeScript when the product needs a useful front end.

blocks.title: Automation & Internal Tools
blocks.body: Small tools that reduce manual work, connect workflows and help teams move faster.

blocks.title: Freelance Projects
blocks.body: Websites, redesigns and custom web apps for people who need practical software, not extra complexity.

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.body: Start with the workflow, the people using it and the constraints around it.

steps.title: Keep it simple
steps.body: Build the smallest useful version before adding more moving parts.

steps.title: Make it reliable
steps.body: Write clear code, keep the system observable and avoid surprises in production.

steps.title: Improve what matters
steps.body: Ship, learn from real use and refine the parts that create value.

meta.title: Freelance IT Consulting | José Miguel Fernández
meta.description: Freelance web design, WordPress, custom software, automation and API integration services for businesses that need to move forward.

schema.name: Freelance IT consulting, web design and automation by José Miguel Fernández
schema.description: Freelance web design, WordPress, custom software, automation, applied AI and API integration services for small businesses and teams.

hub.title: Freelance IT consulting,
hub.intro: I help solo professionals, small businesses, startups and technical teams turn digital problems into concrete solutions: a website that explains the offer better, an internal tool that saves time, an automation that reduces manual work or an integration that connects systems that currently work apart.
hub.bestForText: people looking to hire a freelance developer with technical judgment, direct communication and the ability to take a solution from idea to deployment.
hub.definitionTitle: Technical services for turning a digital problem into a useful solution.
definitionBody: I work where business and technology meet: online presence, custom software, process automation, API integrations and backend systems. The goal is to understand the outcome you need first, then build only what helps achieve it.

That may be a professional website to generate leads, a redesign that clarifies the message, an internal tool to move beyond spreadsheets, an AI automation or an API that connects third-party services with your operation.
hub.advantagesTitle: What a clear technical base gives you.

advantages.title: More clarity when selling
advantages.body: A website or landing page with structure, copy and calls to action designed so visitors quickly understand what you offer and how to contact you.

advantages.title: Less manual work
advantages.body: Automations and integrations that reduce repetitive tasks, copy-paste errors and friction between tools that do not communicate well.

advantages.title: Software that stays maintainable
advantages.body: Applications, APIs and backends built with reasonable scope, organized code and technical decisions that do not block the next phase.

advantages.title: A direct technical partner
advantages.body: You talk to the person who analyzes, proposes and builds. That makes priorities, scope decisions and risk detection easier.

hub.servicesTitle: Services to improve your website, processes and systems.
hub.servicesIntro: You can arrive with a specific need or with a problem that still needs structure. These are the types of work where I usually add the most value.

it-consulting.title: IT consulting and custom software
it-consulting.description: Direct technical help to organize projects, validate decisions and build custom software without inflating the scope.
bullets: Diagnosis of the problem, current workflow and tools your business already uses.
Web applications, APIs, internal panels and integrations with pragmatic architecture.
Technical support for deciding what to build now and what to leave for later.

offerings.title: IT project management
offerings.description: Planning, execution and technical order for websites, applications or integrations.

offerings.title: IT advisory
offerings.description: Help deciding what to build, which technology to use, how much scope it needs and where to start.

offerings.title: Technology second opinion
offerings.description: Review of a website, estimate, architecture or provider before making a decision.

offerings.title: Custom software
offerings.description: Internal tools, APIs, panels and solutions adapted to the real workflow of the business.

offerings.title: API integrations
offerings.description: Connecting websites, CRMs, payments, forms, ERPs, databases and external tools.

offerings.title: Process automation
offerings.description: Reducing manual tasks with automated workflows between tools.

offerings.title: Optimization and maintenance
offerings.description: Performance, security, bug fixing, deployment and stability improvements.

web-wordpress.title: Web design, WordPress and redesign
web-wordpress.description: Professional, editable websites designed to win clients, with a clean, fast and maintainable technical base.
bullets: Clear structure, specific messaging and visible calls to action from the first scroll.
Performance, basic technical SEO, accessibility and maintainability considered from the start.
WordPress when content editing matters; Astro or custom code when that is the better fit.

offerings.title: WordPress web design
offerings.description: Professional, editable websites designed to turn visits into qualified conversations.

offerings.title: WordPress maintenance

offerings.title: WordPress optimization

offerings.title: WordPress migration

offerings.title: Landing pages

ai-automation.title: Automation, applied AI and API integrations
ai-automation.description: Applied AI and process automation to reduce manual work, organize data and connect business tools.
bullets: Practical use cases before shiny demos without daily value.
Automation across APIs, forms, email, documents and internal data.
Human control where judgment matters, traceability where trust matters.

offerings.title: AI for companies
offerings.description: Practical AI use cases applied to real business tasks.

offerings.title: Chatbots and assistants

offerings.title: AI agents

offerings.title: AI training

hub.processTitle: How I work: clarity first, then code.

process.title: Diagnosis
process.body: We clarify the problem, users, current workflow, involved tools and the outcome that should change.

process.title: Useful scope
process.body: I define a small first version that can be built and validated before adding unnecessary complexity or budget.

process.title: Build
process.body: I develop the frontend, backend, automation or integration with clear deliverables, concrete reviews and maintainable code.

process.title: Close
process.body: I validate critical paths, metadata, performance and deployment, then leave notes for operating or extending the work.

useCases: Your website gets visits, but does not explain the offer clearly or turn enough visits into conversations.
Your team runs operations through spreadsheets, emails, forms and copy-paste.
You need a first web application to validate a product or service idea.
Your tools do not talk to each other and force manual data movement between systems.
Your current backend or application is starting to block changes, reliability or maintenance.
hub.recommendationEyebrow: When it makes sense to work with me
hub.recommendationTitle: Who these freelance services are for.
hub.recommendationIntro: I work best when there is a real need already on the table: selling more clearly, saving manual work, connecting systems, improving an old website or launching a first version without building an unnecessary platform.
recommendedFor: Solo professionals, consultants and small businesses that need a clear website to present services and generate contacts.
Teams losing hours to spreadsheets, emails, copy-paste or repetitive tasks that could become a simple internal tool.
Startups, founders or technical leads who need a first application, integration or backend working before investing in a larger team.
notRecommendedFor: Work focused only on visual identity, advertising campaigns or graphic design without technical delivery.
Projects that already need a full product, design, data and operations team from day one.
Ideas that are still too open, without a clear priority or willingness to start with a small, verifiable version.
hub.proofTitle: A close technical profile with product judgment and full-stack execution.
hub.proofText: I do not work like a generic agency. I work as a freelance developer: I listen to the problem, shape the scope and carefully build the website, backend or automation the project needs.
proofPoints: Experience combining frontend, backend, APIs, integrations and production systems.
Judgment to choose between WordPress, Astro, custom applications, automation or Spring Boot depending on the case.
Direct communication, clear deliverables and focus on solutions the business can maintain.
Technical content and personal projects that show how I think before building.
hub.contentTitle: Articles that show how I think when building.

contentLinks.label: When to create an internal tool instead of staying in Excel
Migrating a legacy backend to Spring Boot

hub.faqTitle: Common questions before hiring a technical service.

faq.question: What kind of project can I ask you about?
faq.answer: You can contact me if you need a professional website, a redesign, an internal tool, an automation, an API integration or help structuring a technical decision before investing.

faq.question: Do you work with WordPress or custom code?
faq.answer: I work with both approaches. I use WordPress when comfortable content editing matters, and custom code when the project needs more control, performance, integration or specific logic.

faq.question: Can you improve an existing website or system?
faq.answer: Yes. I can review an existing website, application, backend or integration to find problems with clarity, performance, maintenance, technical SEO or user flow.

faq.question: How do I know if I need an automation or an internal tool?
faq.answer: If you repeat the same task every week, copy data between tools or depend on spreadsheets for critical processes, it is probably worth reviewing whether a small automation or tool could save time.

faq.question: Do you work remotely?
faq.answer: Yes. I work remotely for Spain and other projects that fit by language, time zone and collaboration style.

hub.ctaTitle: Share your case and we can see if it makes sense.
hub.ctaText: Send what you have now, what you want to improve and where time, clarity or money is being lost. I’ll reply with the most reasonable next step.

exitIntent.minutes: min to shape the next step
exitIntent.title: Want me to look at your case before you go?
exitIntent.text: If you have a website, process or idea that feels stuck, send me the context. I will tell you whether it needs a website, automation, integration or something simpler.

detail.pricingNote: Final pricing depends on scope, integrations and project state. If it does not fit, I will say so before estimating.
detail.benefitsTitle: What you can expect to improve
detail.whyMeTitle: Technical judgment, clear scope and direct execution.
detail.contactText: Tell me what you want to build, what you have now and what outcome you need. If it fits, I’ll suggest a clear next step.
detail.contactModalText: Send the project context and I’ll reply with the most reasonable next step.
detail.whatsappMessage: Hi José Miguel, I am looking at the {service} service and would like to tell you about my case.
detail.blogText: I write about backend work, tools, automation and practical decisions while building software.

business-website.title: Business Website
business-website.description: A clean, fast website that explains what you do, builds trust and makes it easy for the right client to contact you.
business-website.metaTitle: Business Website Development - José Miguel Fernández
business-website.metaDescription: Freelance business website development for small teams and solo professionals who need a clear, fast and maintainable website.

pricing.label: Professional website
pricing.note: For service websites, landing pages or a professional online presence with clear structure.
scope: Up to 5 sections or one complete landing page.
Responsive design, base copy and contact form.
Basic technical SEO, performance and deployment included.

overview: This service is for creating or improving a professional web presence when people need to understand quickly what you do, who you help and what they should do next.

It can be a landing page, a service website, an editable WordPress site or a lighter Astro build. The right choice depends on how much content you need to manage, the performance you want and how the website will be maintained afterwards.
business-website.problem: Your current online presence does not explain the offer clearly, or you are relying on scattered profiles instead of a focused website.

benefits.title: Clearer messaging
benefits.body: I organize the offer, sections and calls to action so visitors do not have to guess what you sell or how to contact you.

benefits.title: A better SEO base
benefits.body: The page starts with heading hierarchy, metadata, performance and internal links designed so search engines and AI assistants can understand the service.

benefits.title: More trust before contact
benefits.body: The website presents services, context, proof of work and contact paths in a sober, professional way that works well on mobile.

whenNeeded: You get visits or referrals, but people still ask basic questions about your service.
Your current website no longer represents your professional level or relies too much on social profiles.
You want to validate an offer, service or campaign with a clear page before investing in something larger.
You need a fast, maintainable website ready for future service pages or articles.
whoFor: Freelancers and consultants who need a credible home for their work.
Small businesses that want a simple site clients can understand quickly.
Founders validating a service before investing in a larger product.
builds: Landing pages and small websites with clear sections and CTAs.
Responsive Astro pages that are fast and easy to maintain.
Basic SEO structure, metadata, internal links and deployment notes.
Contact paths that point people to email, LinkedIn or the existing form.
process: Clarify the offer, audience and main action you want visitors to take.
Shape the page structure and copy before writing code.
Build the responsive site using the current stack and keep it simple.
Validate build, links, metadata and mobile layout before handoff.
whyMe: I combine copy, technical structure and development so the site is not only visually clean, but useful for selling and maintaining.
I can work with WordPress when easy editing matters or Astro when performance, control and simplicity matter more.
I keep the scope focused: first a website that explains and converts, then extra complexity if the evidence justifies it.

faq.question: Do I need all the content ready before starting?
faq.answer: No. A rough outline is enough to start. I can help turn it into clear website copy.

faq.question: Can this be expanded later?
faq.answer: Yes. I keep the structure maintainable so service pages, blog posts or product sections can be added later.

website-redesign.title: Website Redesign
website-redesign.description: A focused redesign for an existing website that feels dated, unclear, slow or difficult to update.
website-redesign.metaTitle: Website Redesign for Clearer Conversion - José Miguel Fernández
website-redesign.metaDescription: Freelance website redesign for existing sites that need better structure, performance, messaging and conversion paths.

pricing.label: Focused redesign
pricing.note: For improving an existing website without rebuilding everything from scratch.
scope: Review of structure, messaging, mobile layout and conversion paths.
UI, performance and call-to-action improvements.
Short notes on opportunities outside the agreed scope.

overview: A website redesign is not about changing colors for the sake of it. It is about recovering clarity, performance and trust when an existing website no longer supports the business.
I review structure, messaging, navigation, mobile layout, calls to action and technical issues to decide what deserves attention now and what can wait for a later phase.
website-redesign.problem: Your website exists, but it no longer reflects the quality of your work or makes the next step obvious for visitors.

benefits.title: Less friction for visitors
benefits.body: Navigation, visual hierarchy and CTAs are reorganized so the path to contact, services or useful content becomes clearer.

benefits.title: Better performance and maintenance
benefits.body: I look for unnecessary weight, responsive issues, fragile dependencies and technical details that affect speed, stability or updates.

benefits.title: More value without a full rebuild
benefits.body: I keep what works and focus effort on the parts that block clarity, trust or conversion.

whenNeeded: Your website feels dated, slow or less professional than the quality of your work.
You have added sections over time and the navigation or message has become confusing.
The mobile version is weak or the calls to action are hidden.
You want better SEO, performance and conversion without starting from a blank page.
whoFor: Professionals with an old personal or service website.
Small teams with pages that grew without a clear structure.
Businesses that want better performance and clearer conversion paths.
builds: Information architecture cleanup and stronger page hierarchy.
Responsive layout improvements using the existing visual direction.
Performance, accessibility and SEO fixes where they matter.
Clearer calls to action across home, services, blog and contact.
process: Review the current pages, content, conversion paths and technical issues.
Decide what to keep, remove, rewrite or reorganize.
Implement the redesign without changing the identity from scratch.
Run validation and document any remaining improvement opportunities.
whyMe: I approach redesign through product and technology: messaging, structure, performance and maintenance belong together.
I do not force a full rebrand when it is not needed; I prefer concrete changes that improve experience and contact.
I can work inside an existing Astro, React or WordPress base and keep the work focused.

faq.question: Will you redesign everything from zero?
faq.answer: Not unless that is the right move. I prefer preserving what works and improving the parts that block clarity or trust.

faq.question: Can you work with an existing Astro or React site?
faq.answer: Yes. I can work inside an existing codebase and keep changes focused instead of rebuilding unnecessarily.

custom-web-application.title: Custom Web Application
custom-web-application.description: A focused web application built around a real workflow, with the right amount of frontend, backend and data modeling.
custom-web-application.metaTitle: Custom Web Application Development - José Miguel Fernández
custom-web-application.metaDescription: Freelance custom web application development with TypeScript, backend systems, databases and practical product delivery.

pricing.label: Useful first version
pricing.note: For building a small but real app with workflow, data and deployment.
scope: Scope definition and main screens.
Frontend, backend or integration work depending on the need.
Maintainable base for validating and extending in phases.

overview: A custom web application makes sense when your workflow, product or internal process does not fit well inside an off-the-shelf tool.
The goal is not to build a huge platform from day one. It is to create a useful first version with the screens, data, permissions, backend and integrations needed to validate the real workflow.
custom-web-application.problem: Off-the-shelf tools do not fit the workflow, but a large product build would be too much for the current stage.

benefits.title: A workflow adapted to the business
benefits.body: The application is designed around your users, data and real decisions, not around how a generic tool expects you to work.

benefits.title: A base ready to grow
benefits.body: I define boundaries, models and contracts carefully so the first version can evolve without becoming debt from the start.

benefits.title: Lower product risk
benefits.body: We start with a small, verifiable version so you can learn before investing in extra modules, roles or automation.

whenNeeded: A spreadsheet, form or SaaS tool no longer fits the real workflow.
You need to validate a product idea with users, data and a working experience.
Your team repeats operations that should live in a dedicated panel or workflow.
You want frontend, backend, database and integrations connected without hiring a large team yet.
whoFor: Founders testing a focused product workflow.
Teams replacing spreadsheets or manual coordination with a web app.
Businesses that need a practical tool connected to real data.
builds: Authenticated dashboards, forms, workflows and data views.
Backend APIs, databases and integrations when the app needs them.
Admin areas for managing content, users or operational records.
Deployment, environment notes and a maintainable project structure.
process: Map the workflow, users, permissions and data that matter first.
Define a small first version that can be built and validated.
Build iteratively with clear interfaces between frontend and backend.
Validate the critical paths and leave the codebase ready to evolve.
whyMe: I have full-stack and backend depth, so I can connect interface, data, APIs and deployment without splitting the problem into silos.
I work with small scope and explicit decisions, especially when the product is still validating its shape.
Prioritizing maintainability, permissions, errors and operation helps a useful first version avoid becoming a blocker.

faq.question: Can you build both frontend and backend?
faq.answer: Yes. My strongest base is backend, and I can work across the full stack when that helps ship a useful product.

faq.question: Do you build large platforms?
faq.answer: I prefer starting with a focused version. If the scope is large, I help split it into realistic phases.

internal-tools.title: Internal Tools
internal-tools.description: Small, practical tools for teams that need to manage operations without forcing everything through spreadsheets.
internal-tools.metaTitle: Internal Tool Development - José Miguel Fernández
internal-tools.metaDescription: Freelance internal tool development for teams that need dashboards, admin panels and operational workflows.

pricing.label: Internal tool
pricing.note: For panels, dashboards or internal workflows with real users and data.
scope: Mapping the manual process and smallest useful version.
Private interface, data model and main operations.
Deployment, workflow validation and maintenance notes.

overview: An internal tool is private software that helps a team work better: reviewing requests, managing data, checking statuses, importing files, generating reports or controlling an operational process.
It usually has the most value when it replaces scattered work across spreadsheets, emails and messages with a clear interface, visible rules and data in a more reliable place.
internal-tools.problem: Important work is handled manually across spreadsheets, messages and repeated checks, which makes errors easier and progress harder to see.

benefits.title: More visible operations
benefits.body: The team can see statuses, owners, pending inputs and errors without reconstructing reality across multiple tools.

benefits.title: Fewer repeated mistakes
benefits.body: Important rules become part of the workflow, reducing forgotten steps, duplicates and manual changes that cost time.

benefits.title: A tool shaped around the team
benefits.body: You do not need to buy a large platform if what you need is a concrete, understandable panel connected to your data.

whenNeeded: The team needs to know the status of each case, order, request or review.
Critical operations live in spreadsheets that are no longer easy to audit.
Several people update the same information and errors or duplicates appear.
You need a private panel before investing in a larger internal system.
whoFor: Operations, support or admin teams with repeated manual work.
Small companies that need a private dashboard or admin panel.
Teams that want clarity without buying a complex platform.
builds: Admin panels, dashboards, review queues and simple CRMs.
Role-aware views for internal workflows.
Data import, export and reporting screens.
Small backend services that support daily operations.
process: Understand the current manual process and where mistakes happen.
Define the smallest useful tool with clear input and output.
Build the interface, data model and backend behavior.
Test the real workflow with the people who will use it.
whyMe: I focus on internal tools that reduce real friction, not panels full of features nobody maintains.
I can model data, permissions, interface and backend in a small first version that can actually be operated.
I work with the people who will use the tool to validate the flow before calling it finished.

faq.question: Does an internal tool need to look polished?
faq.answer: It needs to be clear, fast and hard to misuse. Visual polish matters when it helps people work better.

faq.question: Can it connect to existing systems?
faq.answer: Yes, if those systems expose APIs, exports or another stable integration path.

automation-workflows.title: Automation Workflows
automation-workflows.description: Practical automation for repeated work: moving data, sending notifications, generating outputs and keeping systems in sync.
automation-workflows.metaTitle: Automation Workflow Development - José Miguel Fernández
automation-workflows.metaDescription: Freelance automation workflow development for repeated business tasks, integrations, notifications and operational tooling.

pricing.label: Focused automation
pricing.note: For automating one repeated task with clear input, output and visible errors.
scope: Quick analysis of the current manual workflow.
Automation through API, webhook, job or maintainable script.
Testing with real cases and explanation of how it works.

overview: Workflow automation is for making a repeatable task happen consistently: reading data, classifying it, moving it between systems, sending alerts, generating documents or triggering an action.

It can be built with APIs, webhooks, scheduled jobs, maintainable scripts or applied AI when it adds real value. The priority is for the flow to be clear, traceable and easy to correct when something fails.
automation-workflows.problem: People are spending time on repeatable tasks that software can handle more reliably, but the workflow is too specific for a generic tool.

benefits.title: Measurable time savings
benefits.body: I automate tasks with clear input and output to reduce manual hours, especially when they repeat every week.

benefits.title: More visible errors
benefits.body: A good automation does not fail silently: it leaves logs, statuses or alerts so you know what happened and what needs review.

benefits.title: AI where it makes sense
benefits.body: AI can classify, summarize or extract information, while human review stays in place when decisions require judgment.

whenNeeded: You copy the same information between tools manually.
You generate reports, emails, documents or alerts with a repeated structure.
A process gets stuck because nobody sees errors until too late.
You want to test applied AI on a concrete task before redesigning the whole operation.
whoFor: Teams copying data between systems by hand.
Businesses that need reliable notifications, reports or generated files.
Founders who want to remove operational drag from a process.
builds: Scheduled jobs, webhooks and workflow triggers.
Data cleanup, transformation and synchronization scripts.
Notifications through email, Slack-like tools or custom channels.
Simple monitoring and error reporting for critical automations.
process: Map the manual workflow and define what should happen automatically.
Identify the trigger, data source, output and failure cases.
Build the automation with logging and safe fallback behavior.
Test it with real examples before relying on it.
whyMe: I do not automate for the sake of it: first I identify what is repeatable and where human control should remain.
I work with APIs, webhooks, scripts, jobs and applied AI, choosing the most maintainable path for the case.
I care about visible errors, tests with real examples and explaining how the automation works so you are not depending on a black box.

faq.question: Can everything be automated?
faq.answer: No. I focus on the repeatable parts and keep human review where judgment is needed.

faq.question: How do you avoid fragile automations?
faq.answer: I prefer APIs, explicit inputs and visible errors over hidden scripts that silently fail.

api-integrations.title: API Integrations
api-integrations.description: Reliable connections between tools, platforms and internal systems so data can move without manual copying.
api-integrations.metaTitle: API Integration Development - José Miguel Fernández
api-integrations.metaDescription: Freelance API integration development for connecting products, internal tools, data flows and third-party services.

pricing.label: API integration
pricing.note: For connecting two systems or exposing a data flow reliably.
scope: Review of documentation, authentication and limits.
Client, endpoint or webhook with validation and visible errors.
Environment variables and notes for operating the integration.

overview: An API integration connects tools so data can move between systems without manual copying, exporting or reconciliation.
It may involve consuming a third-party API, creating your own endpoints, receiving webhooks, transforming data, syncing states or exposing a small interface to supervise the flow.
api-integrations.problem: Your tools hold useful data, but they do not talk to each other in the way your workflow needs.

benefits.title: More consistent data
benefits.body: The integration reduces duplicates, mismatches and errors when moving information across CRMs, forms, payments, internal tools or products.

benefits.title: Less manual dependency
benefits.body: Important changes can travel through an API or webhook instead of waiting for an export or manual review.

benefits.title: Failure behavior included
benefits.body: I define validation, retries, visible errors and limits so the integration remains operable when something external changes.

whenNeeded: You have two or more tools with related data that do not sync well.
You need to receive webhooks, consume a third-party API or expose data to another system.
The team loses time exporting, pasting or correcting information across platforms.
A commercial or operational flow depends on payments, forms, CRM or panels being connected.
whoFor: Teams connecting CRMs, forms, payments, dashboards or internal systems.
Products that need to consume or expose an API.
Businesses replacing manual copy-paste with a reliable data flow.
builds: REST API clients, server endpoints and webhook receivers.
Data mapping between third-party tools and internal models.
Retry, validation and error handling for important flows.
Documentation for environment variables and operational behavior.
process: Review the API docs, authentication and limits before committing to the flow.
Define the data contract and failure behavior.
Build the integration in a way that is testable and observable.
Validate real requests and document how to maintain it.
whyMe: I work on integrations from the data contract and failure cases, not only from the happy-path API call.
I can build both the backend work and the screens needed to review status, errors or synchronized data.
I document variables, limits and operational behavior so the integration stays maintainable.

faq.question: Can you work with messy API documentation?
faq.answer: Usually, yes. I start by validating the real requests and constraints before building too much around assumptions.

faq.question: Do you only build backend integrations?
faq.answer: No. I can build the backend integration and the frontend screens needed to use or monitor it.

backend-spring-boot.title: Spring Boot Backend
backend-spring-boot.description: Development and evolution of APIs, backend services and integrations with Java and Spring Boot, prioritizing maintainability, testability and reliability.
backend-spring-boot.metaTitle: Spring Boot Backend Development - José Miguel Fernández
backend-spring-boot.metaDescription: Freelance backend development with Java and Spring Boot for APIs, services, integrations, legacy migrations and maintainable systems.

pricing.label: Backend intervention
pricing.note: For an API, integration or backend improvement with a defined technical scope.
scope: Diagnosis of domain, contracts and pain points.
Implementation with structure, tests or critical validation.
Operational notes about errors, deployment and evolution.

overview: Spring Boot backend development fits when you need reliable APIs, services or integrations on top of a maintainable Java base.
It can be a new API, an architecture improvement, an integration with external systems, a gradual migration from legacy or a focused intervention so the backend stops slowing changes down.
backend-spring-boot.problem: The backend supports the business, but each change costs too much, integrations are fragile or the architecture no longer fits the product.

benefits.title: More reliable APIs
benefits.body: Clear contracts, validation, controlled errors and tests reduce surprises when frontend work, integrations or clients depend on the backend.

benefits.title: Better maintainability
benefits.body: A structure with separated responsibilities, useful tests and explicit decisions makes change easier without touching half the system.

benefits.title: Evolution in phases
benefits.body: For legacy or live systems, I prefer focused interventions that reduce risk before large rewrites become hard to justify.

whenNeeded: Every backend change feels risky because it can break integrations or critical flows.
You need a new API, targeted refactor or Java/Spring Boot integration with external systems.
The legacy system is starting to block product work, reliability or maintenance.
You want to improve tests, observability, contracts or deployment without stopping the business.
whoFor: Teams that need to strengthen or evolve an existing Java/Spring Boot backend.
Businesses with a legacy application that is starting to block changes or maintenance.
Founders who need a reliable API for a web application or internal tool.
builds: REST APIs with clear contracts, validation, security and error handling.
Spring Boot services with maintainable structure, tests and separated responsibilities.
Integrations with databases, queues, third-party services and internal processes.
Observability improvements, health checks, Liquibase migrations and operational notes.
process: Understand the domain, data, existing contracts and points where the system hurts.
Define a focused intervention: new API, targeted refactor, integration or phased migration.
Implement with tests, useful logs and explicit decisions around errors and retries.
Validate critical behavior and leave clear notes for operating or evolving the system.
whyMe: My strongest technical base is backend work, APIs, Java, Spring Boot and systems that need to keep running in production.
I think about contracts, data, errors, deployment and maintainability, not only about closing a coding task.
I can connect the backend with frontend work, automations and integrations when the problem needs a full-stack view.

faq.question: Do you only work with Spring Boot?
faq.answer: Spring Boot is my main backend base, but I can connect it with frontend work, databases, queues, external APIs and deployment workflows when the project needs it.

faq.question: Can you help with a legacy migration?
faq.answer: Yes. I prefer phased migrations: identify boundaries, extract valuable flows and reduce risk before rewriting more than needed.
